articles.full_text:
UP reels under severe heatwave condition:Red alert in 10 districts, storms and rain likely across western region
- Copy link
Uttar Pradesh remains in the grip of intense heat and heatwave conditions. Bright sunshine has prevailed across the state since Sunday morning, while slow wind speeds have increased humidity levels and discomfort.
The Meteorological Department has issued a heatwave alert for 54 districts today. Out of these, 10 districts are under red alert, indicating the likelihood of extremely hot and scorching winds.
Thunderstorm and rain alert in western UP
Alongside the heatwave warning, thunderstorm and rain alerts have also been issued for 24 districts of western Uttar Pradesh. According to the weather department, a western disturbance has become active over central Assam. Its impact may be seen in western UP, particularly in districts bordering Delhi.
Officials said cloudy conditions and rainfall activity are likely in these areas.
Banda hottest in UP at 46.2°C
Earlier on Saturday, Banda recorded the highest temperature in the state at 46.2°C.
Other major temperatures recorded included-
Ghaziabad witnessed light rainfall, while dust storms were reported in Mathura, Ayodhya, Barabanki and Gonda.
Heat affects wildlife in tiger reserve
The heatwave conditions were so severe that tigers and elephants at the Amangarh Tiger Reserve in Bijnor were reportedly distressed by the extreme temperatures. The animals spent most of the day moving around near water bodies to cope with the heat.
The Meteorological Department has also predicted the possibility of thunderstorms and rainfall during Nautapa, which begins from Monday. However, weather experts believe temperatures are still likely to remain extremely high during the period, with the average temperature expected to hover around 45°C.
See photos-
Red alert for next three days, says meteorologist
Meteorologist Atul Kumar Singh said-
A red alert for heatwave conditions has been issued for the next three days. After that, an orange alert will remain in effect. A weather disturbance has become active from Punjab to south-west Rajasthan. Its impact will be visible in western Uttar Pradesh. This may lead to thundercloud activity, hailstorms, gusty winds and scattered rainfall in some places.

articles.title: Banda records 48°C amid severe heatwave in Uttar Pradesh - The News Mill
articles.full_text:
Banda in Uttar Pradesh recorded a temperature of 48 degrees Celsius, making it the hottest location in India as a severe heatwave persists across the region. Hospitals are experiencing an increase in heat stroke cases.
Meteorologist Dr Dinesh from Banda University of Agriculture and Technology reported that temperatures have consistently exceeded 45 degrees Celsius for nearly a week, with night temperatures remaining above 30 degrees Celsius. He explained, “It is recorded as the hottest place, with temperatures consistently above 45 degrees Celsius for nearly a week. Night temperatures remain above 30 degrees Celsius, while daytime readings quickly rise from 44 degrees Celsius to 47-48 degrees Celsius. Key reasons include direct sunlight due to proximity to the Tropic of Cancer, clear skies, plateau terrain with low soil moisture, drying rivers, deforestation, and mining.”
Dr Dinesh added, “Hot winds from the northwest further intensify the heat. Rainfall is limited and uneven, with about 900 mm annually. Lack of water raises ground temperatures. Temperatures will hover around 45 degrees Celsius, with heatwave conditions persisting at least until the end of May.”
Dr Vineet Sachan, a physician at the Trauma Centre, noted that hospitals are seeing a significant rise in patient numbers, with an additional 300-400 cases daily over the usual 1,800-2,000. Most patients present symptoms such as fever, vomiting, diarrhoea, stomach pain, headaches, and dizziness.
He stated, “Banda has reported extreme temperatures, 48.2°C yesterday and around 47°C today, with the city in the grip of severe heat. Hospitals are seeing a surge in patients, mainly with stomach pain, vomiting, fever, headaches, and dizziness. Authorities advise people to avoid going out between 10 a.m. and 3 p.m., and if necessary, to carry umbrellas or cloth coverings, drink plenty of water, avoid going out on an empty stomach, and consume more liquids.”
Dr Sachan further clarified, “The number of patients has risen sharply: from the usual 1,800-2,000 daily cases, an additional 300-400 patients are now arriving, mostly suffering from fever, diarrhoea, stomach pain, and dizziness. Officials estimate 200-300 people are being affected by heat-related illness daily.”
District Magistrate Amit Aseri reported that the administration has implemented advanced monitoring of water and electricity supplies, alongside regular coordination with officials. Measures include early school holidays, adjusted timings, and public advisories urging people to avoid venturing out between 11 a.m. and 4 p.m. unless necessary.
He added, “Hospitals and ambulances have been kept on alert to handle heat-related emergencies. Temperatures have crossed 48°C in recent days. The administration had prepared in advance, monitoring water and electricity supply, and collecting reports from officials. Schools were given early holidays, timings were adjusted, and advisories given — urging people not to go out between 11 a.m. and 4 p.m. unless essential, and to wear light clothing. Children and the elderly were advised to stay indoors. Hospitals and ambulances remain on alert.”
Mr Aseri acknowledged concerns about mining but stressed it is conducted with environmental clearance. To mitigate the heat, afforestation drives and tree plantations along rivers and roads are planned. Police and administration jointly enforce regulations and maintain vigilance.
A resident, Anamika Awasthi, described the conditions as unbearable, saying, “Banda is facing extreme heat, with temperatures reaching 48.2°C yesterday and around 47°C today. Stepping outside feels unbearable, with hot winds causing heatstroke. Temperatures have risen higher than ever before, making daily life increasingly difficult.”

articles.title: झुलसते यूपी में रेन अलर्ट की घंटी, इन 26 जिलों का आज कुछ नहीं बिगाड़ पाएगी गर्मी, बांदा में गर्मी फिर सबसे ज्यादा - ETV Bharat
articles.search_term: ("हीट एक्शन प्लान" OR "गर्मी से राहत" OR चेतावनी OR "रेड अलर्ट" OR "ऑरेंज अलर्ट" OR "येलो अलर्ट" OR एडवाइजरी OR "राहत सामग्री" OR "राहत शिविर" OR मुआवजा) Uttar Pradesh
articles.full_text:
लखनऊ: उत्तर प्रदेश में लगातार आसमान से आग बरस रही है. 45 से अधिक जिले भट्टी की तरह तप रहे हैं. ज्यादातर इलाकों में अधिकतम तापमान सामान्य से 2 से लेकर 5 डिग्री सेल्सियस तक अधिक होने के कारण भीषण गर्मी का दंश प्रदेशवासियों को झेलना पड़ रहा है. बांदा लगातार उत्तर प्रदेश का सबसे अधिक गर्म जिला बना हुआ है. बांदा के अलावा बुंदेलखंड के लगभग सभी जिलों में भीषण गर्मी के साथ-साथ हीट वेव चल रही है.
मौसम विज्ञान विभाग ने रविवार को पश्चिमी उत्तर प्रदेश के 26 से अधिक जिलों में गरज चमक के साथ हल्की बारिश होने और बादलों की आवाजाही होने की चेतावनी जारी की है. साथ ही प्रदेश के 60 से अधिक जिलों में हीट वेव चलने की भी चेतावनी जारी की है. मौसम विज्ञान विभाग के अनुसार हल्की बारिश के बावजूद भीषण गर्मी से प्रदेशवासियों को ज्यादा राहत नहीं मिलेगी.
भीषण गर्मी का रेड अलर्ट इन जिलों में:बांदा, चित्रकूट, कौशाम्बी, प्रयागराज, फतेहपुर , प्रतापगढ़, मिर्जापुर, चंदौली, वाराणसी, संत रवि दास नगर और आस पास के क्षेत्र में चेतावनी जारी.
लू चलने की संभावना इन जिलों में अधिक:सोनभद्र, जौनपुर, गाजीपुर, कन्नौज, कानपुर देहात, कानपुर नगर, उन्नाव, रायबरेली, अमेठी, सुल्तानपुर, मथुरा, हाथरस, एटा, आगरा, फिरोजाबाद, मैनपुरी, इटावा, औरैया, जालौन, हमीरपुर, महोबा, झांसी, ललितपुर, आजमगढ़, मऊ, बलिया, सीतापुर, हरदोई, फर्रुखाबाद, लखनऊ, बाराबंकी, अयोध्या, अंबेडकर नगर, सहारनपुर, शामली, मुजफ्फरनगर, बागपत, मेरठ, गाजियाबाद, हापुड़, गौतम बुद्ध नगर, बुलंदशहर, अलीगढ़, कासगंज और आस पास के क्षेत्र में ऑरेंज अलर्ट जारी किया गया है.
रात में गर्मी होने की संभावना इन क्षेत्रों में अधिक:फतेहपुर, बस्ती, सिद्धार्थ नगर, गोंडा, बलरामपुर, श्रावस्ती, बहराइच, लखीमपुर खीरी, सीतापुर, हरदोई, फर्रुखाबाद, कन्नौज, कानपुर देहात, कानपुर नगर, उन्नाव, लखनऊ, बाराबंकी, रायबरेली, अमेठी, अयोध्या, मथुरा, हाथरस, कासगंज, एटा, आगरा, फिरोजाबाद, मैनपुरी, इटावा, औरैया, शाहजहांपुर, जालौन, हमीरपुर, महोबा, झांसी, ललितपुर और आस पास के क्षेत्र में अलर्ट जारी किया गया है.
बादल गरजने-बिजली गिरने की चेतावनी इन जिलों में:सहारनपुर, शामली, मुजफ्फरनगर, बागपत, मेरठ, गाजियाबाद, हापुड़, गौतम बुद्ध नगर, बुलंदशहर, अलीगढ़, मथुरा, हाथरस, कासगंज, एटा, आगरा, फिरोजाबाद, मैनपुरी, इटावा, औरैया, बिजनौर, अमरोहा, मुरादाबाद, रामपुर, संभल और आस पास के क्षेत्र में अलर्ट जारी किया गया है.
ऐसा रहेगा लखनऊ का वेदर:मौसम विज्ञान विभाग के अनुसार रविवार को आसमान साफ रहेगा. तेज धूप खिलेगी, दिन में कुछ स्थानों पर हीट वेव चलने और रात में गर्मी होने की चेतावनी जारी की गई है. अधिकतम तापमान 45 और न्यूनतम तापमान 29 डिग्री सेल्सियस रहने की संभावना है.
बांदा सबसे गर्म जिला:शनिवार को उत्तर प्रदेश का बांदा सबसे अधिक गर्म जिला रहा. यहां पर अधिकतम तापमान 46.2 डिग्री सेल्सियस रिकॉर्ड किया गया जो कि सामान्य से 2.7 डिग्री सेल्सियस अधिक है. वहीं सबसे कम न्यूनतम तापमान बिजनौर जिले में 23.8 डिग्री सेल्सियस रिकॉर्ड किया गया जो कि सामान्य से 1 डिग्री सेल्सियस अधिक है.
मौसम वैज्ञानिक डॉक्टर अतुल सिंह ने बताया कि रविवार को पश्चिमी उत्तर प्रदेश के आइसोलेटेड स्थान पर गरज चमक के साथ हल्की बारिश हो सकती है. मुख्यतः आसमान साफ रहेगा, मौसम ड्राई बना रहेगा. पश्चिमी उत्तर प्रदेश में 25 से 35 किलोमीटर प्रति घंटा की तेज रफ्तार से धूल भरी आंधी चल सकती है.
